forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,60 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: ce80
+title: 'entity-cache 1/4: EntityEvent::EntityChanged carries Vec<FieldChange>; EntityCache computes the diff'
+---
+#entity-cache
+
+Parent design: `01KP65FJHDQ5R2N5C5BJHVHFBF`. This sub-card moves field-level diffing into the entity layer so the cache's broadcast channel carries the same shape the frontend's `entity-field-changed` Tauri event already consumes.
+
+## What
+
+`swissarmyhammer-entity/src/events.rs` currently emits `EntityChanged { entity_type, id, version }` ‚Äî coarse, no field detail. The kanban-app watcher re-derives the diff from raw YAML via `diff_fields` at a higher layer. Move the diff into the cache where both the before-image and after-image live anyway, and make it part of the event payload.
+
+There is an existing `changelog::FieldChange` enum in `swissarmyhammer-entity/src/changelog.rs:48-80` with `Set`/`Removed`/`Changed`/`StringChanged` variants ‚Äî that is the rich shape used for undo/redo. Do **not** reuse it for events; the frontend contract wants a simple `{field, value}`. Introduce a distinct `events::FieldChange` struct.
+
+Files:
+
+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-entity/src/events.rs` ‚Äî add:
+  ```rust
+  pub struct FieldChange {
+      pub field: String,
+      pub value: serde_json::Value,  // Null on removal
+  }
+  ```
+  Change the variant to `EntityChanged { entity_type, id, version, changes: Vec<FieldChange> }`. Keep `serde(tag = "type")` and `Serialize/Deserialize` derives. Document that removals are encoded as `value: Null` (matches the frontend's existing patch semantics).
+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-entity/src/cache.rs` ‚Äî add a private `fn diff(old: Option<&Entity>, new: &Entity) -> Vec<FieldChange>` helper in the cache module (NOT re-exporting from changelog). Logic: walk `new.fields` ‚Äî if key missing from `old` or value differs, emit `{field: key, value: new_value}`. Walk `old.fields` ‚Äî if key missing from `new`, emit `{field: key, value: Null}`. For brand-new entities (`old.is_none()`), emit every `new.fields` entry.
+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-entity/src/cache.rs` ‚Äî `EntityCache::write` (`:152-203`): capture the pre-write cached entity as `old` before `self.inner.write`, compute `changes = diff(old.as_ref(), &canonical)`, emit `EntityChanged { changes }` (only when `changed`). The existing `old_hash` probe at `:154-158` still suppresses no-op writes ‚Äî hash match ‚áí empty changes, no event.
+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-entity/src/cache.rs` ‚Äî `EntityCache::refresh_from_disk` (`:244-274`): same pattern ‚Äî capture the pre-refresh cached entity, diff against the newly-read one, emit with `changes`.
+
+Subtasks:
+
+- [ ] Add `FieldChange` struct + change `EntityChanged` shape in `events.rs`.
+- [ ] Add private `diff` helper in `cache.rs`.
+- [ ] Update `EntityCache::write` and `EntityCache::refresh_from_disk` to compute and emit `changes`.
+- [ ] Update existing `cache.rs` tests that destructure `EntityChanged { entity_type, id, version }` to also handle the `changes` field. Add new diff-specific tests (below).
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] `EntityEvent::EntityChanged` carries `Vec<FieldChange>` (field + value pairs). No-op writes (hash unchanged) emit no event.
+- [ ] Removals are encoded as `{field, value: Null}`.
+- [ ] Brand-new entities (first write, no prior cache entry) emit `changes` containing every field of the new entity.
+- [ ] `changelog::FieldChange` is untouched ‚Äî undo/redo tests stay green.
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-entity` passes including new tests.
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-entity/src/cache.rs` ‚Äî `test_entity_changed_carries_field_diff`: write `{a:1, b:2}`; write `{a:1, b:3, c:4}`; receiver sees `EntityChanged { changes }` with entries for `b` and `c` (any order), no entry for `a`.
+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-entity/src/cache.rs` ‚Äî `test_entity_changed_encodes_removal_as_null`: write `{a:1, b:2}`; write `{a:1}`; receiver sees `EntityChanged { changes: [{b, Null}] }`.
+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-entity/src/cache.rs` ‚Äî `test_entity_changed_new_entity_lists_all_fields`: first write of `{a:1, b:2}`; receiver sees `EntityChanged { changes }` containing both `a` and `b` with their values.
+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-entity/src/cache.rs` ‚Äî update `write_emits_entity_changed_event` (`:465-486`), `write_same_content_no_event` (`:510-524`), `versions_monotonically_increasing` (`:527-550`), `refresh_from_disk_emits_event_on_change` (`:553-570`) to destructure the new `changes` field.
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-entity` ‚Äî full green.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` ‚Äî start with the three new diff-specific tests (they fail because the current event has no `changes` field and won't compile), add the struct + field, then implement the `diff` helper until tests pass.
+
+## Scope / depends_on
+- No depends_on.
+- Subsequent sub-cards depend on this: `entity-cache 2/4` (context wiring), `entity-cache 4/4` (kanban-app bridge collapse) both consume the new event shape.

changes.value: entity-cache 1/4: EntityEvent::EntityChanged carries Vec<FieldChange>; EntityCache computes the diff

#entity-cache

Parent design: `01KP65FJHDQ5R2N5C5BJHVHFBF`. This sub-card moves field-level diffing into the entity layer so the cache's broadcast channel carries the same shape the frontend's `entity-field-changed` Tauri event already consumes.

## What

`swissarmyhammer-entity/src/events.rs` currently emits `EntityChanged { entity_type, id, version }` ‚Äî coarse, no field detail. The kanban-app watcher re-derives the diff from raw YAML via `diff_fields` at a higher layer. Move the diff into the cache where both the before-image and after-image live anyway, and make it part of the event payload.

There is an existing `changelog::FieldChange` enum in `swissarmyhammer-entity/src/changelog.rs:48-80` with `Set`/`Removed`/`Changed`/`StringChanged` variants ‚Äî that is the rich shape used for undo/redo. Do **not** reuse it for events; the frontend contract wants a simple `{field, value}`. Introduce a distinct `events::FieldChange` struct.

Files:

- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-entity/src/events.rs` ‚Äî add:
  ```rust
  pub struct FieldChange {
      pub field: String,
      pub value: serde_json::Value,  // Null on removal
  }
  ```
  Change the variant to `EntityChanged { entity_type, id, version, changes: Vec<FieldChange> }`. Keep `serde(tag = "type")` and `Serialize/Deserialize` derives. Document that removals are encoded as `value: Null` (matches the frontend's existing patch semantics).
- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-entity/src/cache.rs` ‚Äî add a private `fn diff(old: Option<&Entity>, new: &Entity) -> Vec<FieldChange>` helper in the cache module (NOT re-exporting from changelog). Logic: walk `new.fields` ‚Äî if key missing from `old` or value differs, emit `{field: key, value: new_value}`. Walk `old.fields` ‚Äî if key missing from `new`, emit `{field: key, value: Null}`. For brand-new entities (`old.is_none()`), emit every `new.fields` entry.
- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-entity/src/cache.rs` ‚Äî `EntityCache::write` (`:152-203`): capture the pre-write cached entity as `old` before `self.inner.write`, compute `changes = diff(old.as_ref(), &canonical)`, emit `EntityChanged { changes }` (only when `changed`). The existing `old_hash` probe at `:154-158` still suppresses no-op writes ‚Äî hash match ‚áí empty changes, no event.
- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-entity/src/cache.rs` ‚Äî `EntityCache::refresh_from_disk` (`:244-274`): same pattern ‚Äî capture the pre-refresh cached entity, diff against the newly-read one, emit with `changes`.

Subtasks:

- [ ] Add `FieldChange` struct + change `EntityChanged` shape in `events.rs`.
- [ ] Add private `diff` helper in `cache.rs`.
- [ ] Update `EntityCache::write` and `EntityCache::refresh_from_disk` to compute and emit `changes`.
- [ ] Update existing `cache.rs` tests that destructure `EntityChanged { entity_type, id, version }` to also handle the `changes` field. Add new diff-specific tests (below).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `EntityEvent::EntityChanged` carries `Vec<FieldChange>` (field + value pairs). No-op writes (hash unchanged) emit no event.
- [ ] Removals are encoded as `{field, value: Null}`.
- [ ] Brand-new entities (first write, no prior cache entry) emit `changes` containing every field of the new entity.
- [ ] `changelog::FieldChange` is untouched ‚Äî undo/redo tests stay green.
- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-entity` passes including new tests.

## Tests

- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-entity/src/cache.rs` ‚Äî `test_entity_changed_carries_field_diff`: write `{a:1, b:2}`; write `{a:1, b:3, c:4}`; receiver sees `EntityChanged { changes }` with entries for `b` and `c` (any order), no entry for `a`.
- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-entity/src/cache.rs` ‚Äî `test_entity_changed_encodes_removal_as_null`: write `{a:1, b:2}`; write `{a:1}`; receiver sees `EntityChanged { changes: [{b, Null}] }`.
- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-entity/src/cache.rs` ‚Äî `test_entity_changed_new_entity_lists_all_fields`: first write of `{a:1, b:2}`; receiver sees `EntityChanged { changes }` containing both `a` and `b` with their values.
- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-entity/src/cache.rs` ‚Äî update `write_emits_entity_changed_event` (`:465-486`), `write_same_content_no_event` (`:510-524`), `versions_monotonically_increasing` (`:527-550`), `refresh_from_disk_emits_event_on_change` (`:553-570`) to destructure the new `changes` field.
- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-entity` ‚Äî full green.

## Workflow
- Use `/tdd` ‚Äî start with the three new diff-specific tests (they fail because the current event has no `changes` field and won't compile), add the struct + field, then implement the `diff` helper until tests pass.

## Scope / depends_on
- No depends_on.
- Subsequent sub-cards depend on this: `entity-cache 2/4` (context wiring), `entity-cache 4/4` (kanban-app bridge collapse) both consume the new event shape.
